title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: Welcome mtaOpenData package, R package dedicated helping R users connect NY State Open Data portal! package follows design principles nycOpenData nysOpenData. mtaOpenData package provides streamlined interface accessing MTA-related datasets NY State Open Data portal. two ways:

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: primary way pull data package mta_pull_dataset() function, works tandem mta_list_datasets(). need know anything API keys authentication. first step call mta_list_datasets() see datasets list available use mta_pull_dataset() function. provides information thousands datasets found portal. output includes columns dataset title, description, link source. important pieces key uid. need either order use mta_pull_dataset() function. can supply either key uid dataset argument. instance, want pull dataset MTA Bus Stops, can use either methods : matter put uid key value dataset =, successfully get data!
code: mta_list_datasets() |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 12 #>   dataset_title    open_dataset_id key   status plan_submission_date description #>   <chr>            <chr>           <chr> <chr>  <chr>                <chr>       #> 1 MTA Subway and … 23fs-vfbd       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             This datas… #> 2 MTA Bus Stops    2ucp-7wg5       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             This datas… #> 3 MTA Rail Branch… 2vcb-zrh4       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             This datas… #> 4 MTA Bridges and… 2wqd-qady       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             This datas… #> 5 MTA Workplace V… 2xh4-m2qk       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             This datas… #> 6 MTA Subway Serv… 32ch-sei3       mta_… PUBLI… COMPLETE             Service De… #> # ℹ 6 more variables: plan_submission_date_notes <chr>, type <chr>, #> #   agency <chr>, posting_frequency <chr>, dataset_created_at <chr>, #> #   nys_url_url <chr> mta_bus_stops_id <- mta_pull_dataset(   dataset = "2ucp-7wg5", limit = 2)  mta_bus_stops_key <- mta_pull_dataset(   dataset = "mta_bus_stops", limit = 2)

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: easiest workflow use mta_list_datasets() together mta_pull_dataset(). However, ample datasets portal, new ones added time, list datasets. event particular dataset want use R list, can use mta_any_dataset(). requirement dataset’s API endpoint (URL provided MTA Open Data portal). steps get : NY State Open Data Portal, go dataset want work . Click “Export” (next actions button right hand side). Click “API Endpoint”. Click “SODA2” “Version”. Copy API Endpoint. example use mta_any_dataset() API endpoint discovered, pull data mta_pull_dataset() example:

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: functions provide access NY State Open Data, serve slightly different purposes. general: Use mta_pull_dataset() dataset available mta_list_datasets() Use mta_any_dataset() working datasets outside catalog Together, functions allow users either quickly access datasets flexibly query dataset available MTA section NYS Open Data portal.

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: NYC city never sleeps, also city ’s always moving. Mass Transit Authority (MTA) NYC responsible millions commuters every single day. bus system quite intricate, bus stop information can found . R, mtaOpenData package can used pull data directly. Let’s filter dataset include rows route_id “QM15”, bus traverses Queens Manhattan. mta_pull_dataset() function can filter based columns dataset. filter, add filters = list() put whatever filters like inside. colnames call , know column called “route_id” can use accomplish . Success! calling qm15_info dataset see 2 rows data, distinct() call see qm15_info featured dataset “QM15.” can also add one criteria filtering. successfully filtered : latest two rows route either “QM15” “QM17” latest two rows route either “QM15” “QM17” effect
code: qm15_info <- mta_pull_dataset(dataset = "2ucp-7wg5",limit = 2, filters = list(route_id = "QM15")) qm15_info #> # A tibble: 2 × 22 #>   valid_from          valid_to            in_effect route_id route_short_name #>   <dttm>              <dttm>              <lgl>     <chr>    <chr>            #> 1 2019-10-01 00:00:00 2019-11-11 00:00:00 FALSE     QM15     QM15             #> 2 2021-11-15 00:00:00 2021-12-19 00:00:00 FALSE     QM15     QM15             #> # ℹ 17 more variables: route_long_name <chr>, route_description <chr>, #> #   route_color <chr>, stop_id <dbl>, stop_name <chr>, direction_id <dbl>, #> #   direction <chr>, revenue_stop <dbl>, timepoint <dbl>, boarding <dbl>, #> #   alighting <dbl>, is_cbd <lgl>, latitude <dbl>, longitude <dbl>, #> #   bundle <chr>, georeference_type <chr>, georeference_coordinates <list>  # Checking to see the filtering worked qm15_info |>   distinct(route_id) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   route_id #>   <chr>    #> 1 QM15 qm15_17_info <- mta_pull_dataset(dataset = "2ucp-7wg5",limit = 2, filters = list(route_id = c("QM15","QM17"))) qm15_17_info #> # A tibble: 2 × 22 #>   valid_from          valid_to            in_effect route_id route_short_name #>   <dttm>              <dttm>              <lgl>     <chr>    <chr>            #> 1 2019-10-01 00:00:00 2019-11-11 00:00:00 FALSE     QM15     QM15             #> 2 2021-11-15 00:00:00 2021-12-19 00:00:00 FALSE     QM15     QM15             #> # ℹ 17 more variables: route_long_name <chr>, route_description <chr>, #> #   route_color <chr>, stop_id <dbl>, stop_name <chr>, direction_id <dbl>, #> #   direction <chr>, revenue_stop <dbl>, timepoint <dbl>, boarding <dbl>, #> #   alighting <dbl>, is_cbd <lgl>, latitude <dbl>, longitude <dbl>, #> #   bundle <chr>, georeference_type <chr>, georeference_coordinates <list> qm15_17_info_in_effect <- mta_pull_dataset(dataset = "2ucp-7wg5",limit = 2, filters = list(route_id = c("QM15","QM17"), in_effect = "true")) qm15_17_info_in_effect #> # A tibble: 2 × 21 #>   valid_from          valid_to            in_effect route_id route_short_name #>   <dttm>              <dttm>              <lgl>     <chr>    <chr>            #> 1 2026-01-21 00:00:00 2026-03-27 00:00:00 TRUE      QM17     QM17             #> 2 2026-01-21 00:00:00 2026-03-27 00:00:00 TRUE      QM15     QM15             #> # ℹ 16 more variables: route_long_name <chr>, route_color <chr>, stop_id <dbl>, #> #   stop_name <chr>, direction_id <dbl>, direction <chr>, revenue_stop <dbl>, #> #   timepoint <dbl>, boarding <dbl>, alighting <dbl>, is_cbd <lgl>, #> #   latitude <dbl>, longitude <dbl>, bundle <chr>, georeference_type <chr>, #> #   georeference_coordinates <list>

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: Now successfully pulled data R, let’s mini analysis using direction column, exploring directional patterns active bus routes. , create bar graph directions Distribution active NYC bus route directions. Data retrieved via mtaOpenData package MTA Bus Route info dataset (ID: 2ucp-7wg5). graph shows us directions buses travel current routes!
code: # Visualizing the distribution, ordered by frequency  mta_bus_info <- mta_pull_dataset(dataset = "2ucp-7wg5", filters = list(in_effect = "true"))  mta_bus_info |>   count(direction) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = n,     y = reorder(direction, n)   )) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Top Directions NYC Buses Go On Their Routes",     x = "Count",     y = "Direction"   )

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: mtaOpenData package serves robust interface NY State Open Data portal, streamlining path raw city APIs actionable insights. abstracting complexities data acquisition—pagination, type-casting, complex filtering—allows users focus analysis rather data engineering. demonstrated vignette, package provides seamless workflow targeted data retrieval, automated filtering, rapid visualization.

title: Getting Started with mtaOpenData
text: use package research educational purposes, please cite follows: Martinez C (2026). mtaOpenData: Convenient Access MTA Open Data API Endpoints. R package version 0.1.0, https://martinezc1.github.io/mtaOpenData/.

what: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
title: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
text: Downloads MTA Open Data dataset given Socrata JSON endpoint.

title: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
code: mta_any_dataset(   json_link,   limit = 10000,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
text: json_link Socrata dataset JSON endpoint URL (e.g., "https://data.ny.gov/resource/2ucp-7wg5.json"). limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
text: tibble containing requested dataset.

title: Load Any MTA Open Data Dataset — mta_any_dataset
code: # Examples that hit the live MTA Open Data API are guarded so CRAN checks # do not fail when the network is unavailable or slow. if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   endpoint <- "https://data.ny.gov/resource/2ucp-7wg5.json"   out <- try(mta_any_dataset(endpoint, limit = 3), silent = TRUE)   if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) {     head(out)   } }

what: List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets
title: List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets
text: Retrieves current MTA Open Data catalog returns datasets available use `mta_pull_dataset()`.

title: List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets
List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets
text: tibble available datasets, including generated `key`, dataset   `uid`, dataset `dataset_title`.

title: List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets
text: Keys generated dataset names using `janitor::make_clean_names()`.

title: List datasets available in mtaOpenData — mta_list_datasets

what: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
text: Uses dataset `key` `open_dataset_id` `mta_list_datasets()` pull data MTA Open Data.

title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
code: mta_pull_dataset(   dataset,   limit = 10000,   filters = list(),   date = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   date_field = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
text: dataset dataset key open_dataset_id `mta_list_datasets()`. limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). filters Optional named list filters. Supports vectors (translated ()). date Optional single date (matches times day) using `date_field`. Optional start date (inclusive) using `date_field`. Optional end date (exclusive) using `date_field`. date_field Optional date/datetime column use `date`, ``, ``. Must supplied `date`, ``, `` used. Optional raw SoQL clause. `date`, ``, `` provided, conditions -ed . order Optional SoQL ORDER clause. timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
text: tibble.

title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
text: Dataset keys generated dataset_title using `janitor::make_clean_names()`. keys derived live catalog metadata, dataset open_dataset_ids stable option.

title: Pull a MTA Open Data dataset from the MTA Open Data catalog — mta_pull_dataset
code: if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   # Pull by key   mta_pull_dataset("mta_bus_stops", limit = 3)    # Pull by open_dataset_id   mta_pull_dataset("2ucp-7wg5", limit = 3)    # Filters   mta_pull_dataset("2ucp-7wg5", limit = 3, filters = list(route_id = "QM3"))  }
